pm_research_plan: **Problem:** An aspiring education entrepreneur wants to open a tuition centre in Sungai Petani, Kedah, but lacks a structured, evidence-backed understanding of which strategies, locations, competitors, pricing models, and regulatory requirements will determine whether the centre can attract and sustain enough students to be viable. **Target Users:** Primary: Parents of primary/secondary school students (Standard 1–Form 5) in Sungai Petani seeking academic improvement. Secondary: Students themselves, especially those preparing for national exams (UPSR replacement assessments, SPM, STPM). Tertiary: Adult learners or niche segments (e.g., English enrichment, coding, religious studies). **MVP Goal:** Validate whether a single-location tuition centre in Sungai Petani can achieve a minimum of 50 paying students within the first 3 months of operation, at a price point that covers variable cost ... [compressed 4240 chars] ... esidential house vs. shared space) minimizes risk while allowing proof of concept? **Decision Criteria:** Location decisions should be judged by proximity to target schools and residential density, not just rental cost. Pricing should be validated against competitor benchmarks and parent willingness-to-pay, not set arbitrarily. Subject offerings should prioritize proven demand (exam subjects) over personal teaching preferences. Regulatory compliance is non-negotiable before any marketing begins. The MVP should test one location, two to three subjects, and one pricing tier before any expansion.
